title: verbascum × divaricatum
intro: Verbascum × divaricatum is a hybrid plant species derived from the common mullein (Verbascum thapsus) and the upright mullein (Verbascum virgatum), known for its large, showy yellow flowers and velvety leaves. The plant is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The medicinal properties of Verbascum × divaricatum are said to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as soothe skin irritations and wounds. It can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a versatile remedy for overall well-being.
